# UC-22.2.54 · NIS2 Art.31-35 supervision, enforcement, fine exposure and GDPR coordination evidence

## Description

Surfaces overdue supervisory audit findings and non-closed ServiceNow GRC policy tasks whose descriptions reference inspections or articles so legal can see evidence request backlogs before penalties or GDPR overlaps escalate.

## Value

Executives maintain an operational cockpit for authority interactions—response SLAs, open remediation, and linked privacy incidents—without claiming that any Splunk panel replaces counsel’s interpretation of fines or jurisdiction.

## Implementation

(1) Land `grc:audit_finding` via your GRC tool’s connector. (2) Map ServiceNow policy table through Splunkbase 1928. (3) Optional: ingest DPO breach tickets to `index=privacy`. (4) Daily alert; route to GC office. (5) Quarterly export to `index=audit_evidence`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• GRC platform (ServiceNow GRC, Archer, etc.) streaming structured events to `index=grc` with stable `sourcetype=grc:audit_finding` mapping.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) configured for `sn_grc_policy` or equivalent; modular input interval 300 seconds, credentials in `passwords.conf`.
• Roles separating `privacy` data from general SOC; consider field masking.
• Lookup `nis2_competent_authorities.csv` for human-readable authority metadata (optional enrichment).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Validate API path such as `/api/now/table/sn_grc_policy` returns `number`, `short_description`, `state`, `due_date`. For GRC findings, ensure JSON includes `remediation_status`. If GDPR correlation needed, ingest DPA tickets with `breach_id`. Route all reviewer notes ONLY to `index=audit_evidence` with restrictive retention (example 7 years).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Schedule daily at 07:00 with a 60-day lookback for supervisory context.

```spl
(index=grc sourcetype=grc:audit_finding earliest=-60d)
OR (index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy earliest=-60d)
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval supervision_gap=if(sourcetype="grc:audit_finding" AND remediation_status IN ("open","in_progress") AND isnotnull(due_epoch) AND now()>due_epoch,1,0)
| eval policy_gap=if(sourcetype="snow:sn_grc_policy" AND state!="6" AND match(short_description,"(?i)supervisory|inspect|nis2"),1,0)
| where supervision_gap=1 OR policy_gap=1
```

Use `lookup nis2_supervision_kfp_exceptions` for counsel-approved holds. Throttle by `row_key=coalesce(finding_id,number)`.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick closed historical finding: ensure it does not appear. Open overdue sample from sandbox should match GRC UI. Cross-check ServiceNow record `state` codes versus lookup table.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Executive dashboard tile: count overdue by MS member state code. Webhook to legal slack channel with least data (`finding_id` only). Attach runbook describing when to escalate to outside counsel.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Null due dates** — default ingestion mapping; fix at source.
• **Regex too broad on tasks** — tune `match` expression after false positives; move keywords to lookup.
• **Index latency** — if `_indextime-_time` > 3600, increase poll frequency.
• **Privilege risk** — limit dashboard to `legal_ops` role only.

## SPL

```spl
(index=grc sourcetype=grc:audit_finding earliest=-60d)
       OR (index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy earliest=-60d)
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval supervision_gap=if(sourcetype="grc:audit_finding" AND remediation_status IN ("open","in_progress") AND isnotnull(due_epoch) AND now()>due_epoch,1,0)
| eval policy_gap=if(sourcetype="snow:sn_grc_policy" AND state!="6" AND match(short_description,"(?i)supervisory|inspect|nis2"),1,0)
| where supervision_gap=1 OR policy_gap=1
| eval row_key=coalesce(finding_id,number)
| lookup nis2_supervision_kfp_exceptions row_key OUTPUT exception_until
| where isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| table _time finding_id competent_authority due_date remediation_status number short_description supervision_gap policy_gap
```

## Visualization

SLA breach table by `competent_authority`, timeline of tasks, heatmap of GDPR-tagged linked records.
